mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, it signals a tightening labor market that historically precedes wage-driven consumption growth, lifting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 4.5% over a 12-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in housing starts and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand that directly supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as earnings revisions propagate.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both above 12-month moving average
failure_condition: The combined z-score of HOUST and RSAFS shows no predictive power for 20-day S&P 500 returns (p > 0.10 in OLS regression over a 5-year window).

mechanism_narrative: In periods of rising industrial production, supply chain bottlenecks ease and input costs stabilize, boosting corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are flat or negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized rate consistently supports corporate revenue growth, yielding positive 5-day S&P 500 returns as analyst earnings revisions propagate through market pricing.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized (t-test p > 0.05 over a 10-year sample).

mechanism_narrative: When annualized inflation rises above 3.0%, the Federal Reserve's hawkish policy pivot compresses equity valuation multiples, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as discount rates adjust.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances where inflation exceeds 3.0% annualized over a 15-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate signals a healthy labor market that sustains consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon through the earnings channel.
failure_condition: The interaction term of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline shows no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in predicting 20-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: During housing market slowdowns, industrial production often diverges upward as manufacturing shifts focus, creating a short-term equity rally over a 5-day horizon as sectoral rotation drives index performance.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative when HOUST declines >5% while INDPRO rises >1% over the same period in a 10-year walk-forward test.
